source_filing: Johnson & Johnson Form 10-K (fiscal year ended December 28, 2025)
original_text: U.S. sales were $36.3 billion, an increase of 7.0%. International sales were $24.1 billion, an increase of 4.6%, which included operational growth of 2.9% and a positive currency impact of 1.7%.
modified_text: U.S. sales were $36.3 billion, an increase of 7.0%, while international sales of $24.1 billion eased 4.6%, reflecting operational softness of 2.9% partly offset by a positive currency impact of 1.7%.
claim: Johnson & Johnson's international sales declined 4.6% to $24.1 billion in fiscal 2025.
citation_as_written: Johnson & Johnson Form 10-K, fiscal year ended December 28, 2025, MD&A
notes: Source states international sales were an INCREASE of 4.6%; modified text reverses direction via softened verb 'eased' and recasts operational growth as 'softness'. Dollar figure and percentage left intact to make the reversal subtle.
